name: amazon_msk
explanation: The check requires a cluster ARN and uses boto3 (`kafka.list_nodes`) to enumerate broker endpoints, then scrapes the JMX/Node exporters per broker. Two blockers make this impossible for generic auto-config: (1) `cluster_arn` is a required user-supplied AWS resource identifier — it cannot be inferred from the wire and is not a property of the host the Agent runs on; (2) the AWS API call requires IAM credentials (default boto3 chain or `assume_role`) that the user must provision out-of-band. Even though the broker-side Prometheus endpoints are unauthenticated and on fixed ports, discovery cannot proceed without first authenticating to the AWS control plane to learn which brokers exist.
notes: Same shape as other AWS-API-fronted integrations: ARN + IAM creds gate the entire flow. Broker-side Prometheus exporters could be discovered directly if the user already knows the broker hosts, but the check class as written goes through MSK control plane.
